report_name: Hims & Hers Data Breach: ShinyHunters Steals Support Tickets via Compromised Zendesk Access

threat_actors.name: ShinyHunters
threat_actors.description: A well-known data extortion group that gained unauthorized access to Hims & Hers' Zendesk instance using a compromised Okta SSO account, exfiltrating customer support tickets.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: The attackers gained initial access to the company's Zendesk instance by using a compromised Okta single sign-on (SSO) account.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration to Cloud Storage
attack_patterns.description: The ShinyHunters group exfiltrated millions of customer support tickets to their own infrastructure for extortion purposes.

attack_patterns.name: Supply Chain Compromise
attack_patterns.description: The attack represents a supply chain compromise where the attackers targeted a third-party SaaS provider (Zendesk) to access the primary victim's (Hims & Hers) data via a compromised identity provider account (Okta).

identities.name: Hims & Hers Health
identities.description: A major telehealth provider that was the primary target of the data breach. Customer support tickets were exfiltrated from its third-party customer service platform.

identities.name: Zendesk
identities.description: The third-party customer service SaaS platform used by Hims & Hers. The attackers gained unauthorized access to the Hims & Hers instance on this platform.

identities.name: Okta
identities.description: The identity provider whose single sign-on (SSO) account was compromised and used by the attackers to gain access to the Zendesk instance.

infrastructure.name: ShinyHunters Extortion Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: Infrastructure used by the ShinyHunters group to store the exfiltrated customer support tickets for extortion.

indicators.name: Anomalous Zendesk Ticket Export
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous Zendesk Ticket Export
id: 9c7b5e8a-1f3a-4d6b-8e2f-9a1b3c4d5e6f
status: experimental
description: Detects bulk ticket export activity from Zendesk via API, which could indicate data exfiltration as seen in the Hims & Hers breach.
references:
  - https://vertexaisearch.cloud.google.com/grounding-api-redirect/AUZIYQE_BKshyISdx7rbWzVvDPaXf4nd1sy6iKggKI6zyfaHNvWo9pD4aU6Ucw-5E6Phslyca_CEDk_o79uFYHveznvXuEJsz4Wa7lsV3AmpglFVL0pnxIq4MQLNI0FJ7mTKp5rwrC9OGMrHkM_xFGRoJRn4CGExTyIwIDmsOjlucjVepMAP2-tl_Rw_whaUKFBdI2tVd6T2PQ==
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-05
tags:
  - attack.t1567.002
  - attack.exfiltration
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate administrative actions involving bulk data export for backup or migration.
level: medium
logsource:
  category: webserver
detection:
  selection:
    cs-uri-stem|contains: '/api/v2/tickets/export'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the use of the Zendesk API endpoint for exporting tickets, which was likely used by ShinyHunters to exfiltrate data in bulk. This activity is anomalous when not performed by authorized administrators.

indicators.name: Suspicious Okta SSO Login
indicators.pattern:
title: Okta SSO Login from TOR Exit Node
id: a4d3e2b1-c0a9-4f8d-b7e6-a1b2c3d4e5f6
status: experimental
description: Detects a successful Okta Single Sign-On (SSO) login originating from a TOR exit node. This is highly suspicious and could indicate a compromised account, a precursor to SaaS application compromise.
references:
  - https://vertexaisearch.cloud.google.com/grounding-api-redirect/AUZIYQE_BKshyISdx7rbWzVvDPaXf4nd1sy6iKggKI6zyfaHNvWo9pD4aU6Ucw-5E6Phslyca_CEDk_o79uFYHveznvXuEJsz4Wa7lsV3AmpglFVL0pnxIq4MQLNI0FJ7mTKp5rwrC9OGMrHkM_xFGRoJRn4CGExTyIwIDmsOjlucjVepMAP2-tl_Rw_whaUKFBdI2tVd6T2PQ==
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-05
tags:
  - attack.t1078
  - attack.initial_access
  - attack.t1090.003
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate use of TOR for privacy, though rare in corporate environments for SSO.
level: high
logsource:
  product: okta
  service: okta
detection:
  selection:
    eventtype: 'user.session.start'
    outcome.result: 'SUCCESS'
    security_context.is_tor: 'true'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects suspicious Okta Single Sign-On (SSO) logins, such as those from anonymizing proxies or exhibiting impossible travel patterns, which could indicate the initial account compromise.
